sentences.en: For today's lesson, our teacher took the whole class to the school library to start a pen-pal writing project.
sentences.zh: 今天這堂課，老師帶全班去學校圖書館，開始一個筆友寫信計畫。

sentences.en: Each of us has to write a real paper letter to a student at another school, put it in an envelope, and mail it ourselves.
sentences.zh: 我們每個人都要寫一封真正的紙本信給另一所學校的學生，裝進信封，然後自己寄出去。

sentences.en: The librarian showed us how to find a word's exact meaning in a huge, heavy dictionary instead of just guessing.
sentences.zh: 圖書館員教我們怎麼在一本又厚又重的字典裡找到單字的確切意思，而不是隨便亂猜。

sentences.en: Bernice tried to define the word "diary" out loud before checking, and her guess was somehow both confident and completely wrong.
sentences.zh: Bernice 在查之前就自己大聲定義了「diary」這個字，她的猜測既很有自信又完全猜錯。

sentences.en: Our teacher gave us one clear example: describe your school, your town, and one small daily habit, nothing more complicated than that.
sentences.zh: 老師給了我們一個明確的例子：描述你的學校、你住的鎮，還有一個小小的日常習慣，就這樣，不用太複雜。

sentences.en: I wrote my letter carefully, making sure to put a dot at the end of every single sentence like the librarian reminded us to.
sentences.zh: 我很仔細地寫我的信，確保每一句話結尾都像圖書館員提醒我們的一樣加上句點。

sentences.en: William, who cannot write letters yet at his own school, still wanted to add a drawing to my envelope for good luck.
sentences.zh: William 在他自己的學校還不會寫信，但他還是想在我的信封上加一張圖，圖個好運。

sentences.en: I let him draw a tiny robot on the corner of the envelope, and honestly, it made the whole letter look more interesting.
sentences.zh: 我讓他在信封角落畫了一個小小的機器人，老實說，這讓整封信看起來更有趣了。

sentences.en: Before mailing it, I had to double-check the mailing address on the front, since the librarian said one wrong number means the letter never arrives.
sentences.zh: 寄出之前，我得仔細確認正面的收件地址，因為圖書館員說只要錯一個數字，信就永遠寄不到。

sentences.en: Bernice, of course, spelled her own school's name wrong on her very first attempt and had to start the whole envelope over.
sentences.zh: Bernice 當然在第一次嘗試時就把自己學校的名字拼錯了，只好整個信封重來一次。

sentences.en: We also spent some time reading a real newspaper from that day, since the librarian said letters and news articles should both be clear and simple.
sentences.zh: 我們還花了一些時間讀當天的一份真正的報紙，因為圖書館員說信件跟新聞報導都應該要寫得清楚簡單。

sentences.en: One newspaper article's topic was about a local school building a brand-new library, which felt strangely appropriate for the day.
sentences.zh: 有一篇報紙文章的主題是一所當地學校要蓋一座全新的圖書館，這個主題感覺很應景。

sentences.en: After finishing our letters, we walked as a class to the small post office near school to send our envelopes and one small package of class photos.
sentences.zh: 寫完信之後，我們全班一起走到學校附近的小郵局，寄出我們的信封，還有一小包班級合照的包裹。

sentences.en: The post office worker used a small tool to press an official stamp mark onto every single envelope before it went into a large bag.
sentences.zh: 郵局的工作人員用一個小工具在每個信封上蓋上正式的郵戳，才把信放進一個大袋子裡。

sentences.en: Bernice asked how long the letter would take to arrive, and the worker guessed about three or four days, depending on the address.
sentences.zh: Bernice 問信要花多久才會到，工作人員猜大概三、四天，要看地址而定。

sentences.en: I actually cannot wait to see if some other student, somewhere else, will write back and describe their own ordinary day, just like mine.
sentences.zh: 我真的很期待，看看某個地方的某個學生會不會回信，描述他們自己平凡的一天，就像我一樣。

quiz.question: What does the librarian teach the class to use instead of guessing a word's meaning?
What mistake does Bernice make when addressing her envelope?
options: She spells her own school's name wrong.
She uses the wrong color pen.

quiz.question: How long does the post office worker guess the letter will take to arrive?
